safe_claims.claim: Adaptive dense+HKVM controller improves over ColBERTv2 on MuSiQue, 2Wiki, and HotpotQA under the train-to-dev protocol.
Adaptive dense+HKVM controller improves over learned HKVM calibration on MuSiQue, 2Wiki, and HotpotQA under the train-to-dev protocol.
The paper should describe HKVM-RAG as a dense-aware key-value evidence controller, not only as a standalone structured retriever.
safe_claims.evidence: Dense-only and HKVM-only controls do not fully explain the combined controller gain, while fixed-substrate HotpotQA blocks a standalone-retrieval overclaim.

unsafe_claims.claim: The controller result proves broad dense retriever family generality.
unsafe_claims.reason: ColBERTv2 is the primary paper-facing dense source; BM25 and Contriever are source-level diagnostics.

unsafe_claims.claim: Standalone WHG-KV is always stronger than dense retrieval.
unsafe_claims.reason: HotpotQA fixed-substrate results show the opposite boundary for standalone answer F1.
